title: Why solo founders with AI are winning YC now
post:
YC's latest batch has more solo founders than any batch in history.

That's not a coincidence. It's a structural shift.

Solo founders used to be at a disadvantage: they couldn't code fast enough, ship enough features, or cover enough ground. AI levels that playing field.

A solo founder with AI can now build, market, and support a product that previously required a team of five. And they're showing up to YC interviews with real revenue, not just demos.

VCs are noticing. A founder with $50K MRR and no team is more impressive than a founder with $50K MRR and 10 employees burning $400K/month.

The solo founder isn't just viable anymore — in many cases, they're preferred.

Are we heading toward a world where the best founders are individuals, not teams? Thoughts?
